THE MATTER IS CONTINUED DUE TO A FEDERAL STAY IN BANKRUPTCY COURT. COUNSEL IS TO ADVISE THE COURT OF ANY MOVEMENT AS TO GEORGIA-PACIFIC LLC. DISCUSSIONS ARE HELD AS TO 524G AND THE TEXAS TWO-STEP CONTROVERSY. MS. LETSCH STATES THAT THEY ARE FIGHTING FOR A RETURN OF THESE MATTERS TO THE TORT SYSTEM AND THAT IS WHY THESE CASES ARE LEFT ON THE COURT'S CALENDAR. MS. CHU STATES THAT IF THEY DISMISS THE CASE THERE ARE CONCERNS AS TO WHETHER OR NOT THEY WILL BE ABLE TO COME BACK AND THAT THERE WILL BE A PREJUDICE TO THEIR CLIENTS. THE COURT STATES THAT GOING FORWARD IT WOULD LIKE PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT ONE LETTER TO THE COURT AT LEAST EVERY SIX MONTHS. THE LETTER IS TO STATE IF A PLAN HAS BEEN PROPOSED, IS TO PROVIDE WHAT THE TIMING OF THE PLAN WILL BE, AND IS TO INCLUDE AN UPDATE ON THE CURRENT STATUS OF THE BANKRUPTCY. KIMBERLY CHU, ESQ., AND BRYN LETSCH, ESQ., OF BRAYTON PURCELL APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA ZOOM. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: J. ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. (304)

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Kehoe Plastering Company, Inc., Paramount Lath and Plaster Co. and RDI Corporation (f/k/a Reeder Development Corporation).
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Michael Shintaku, CSR #8251. Telephone: 408-410-8040. Email: emailformike@hotmail.com.

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Nathan Talley, who was not present for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants Kehoe Plastering Company, Inc., Paramount Lath and Plaster Co. and RDI Corporation (f/k/a Reeder Development Corporation).
Plaintiff's Complaint for Personal Injury-Asbestos was filed on October 10, 2017.
Amendment to Complaint substituting Kehoe Plastering Company, Inc. for DOE #301, and Paramount Lath & Plastering Co. for DOE #302, was filed on March 8, 2018.
Plaintiff's Summons, Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages was personally served on defendant Kehoe Plastering Company, Inc.'s Agent of Process on April 6, 2018.
Plaintiff's Summons Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages was personally served on defendant Paramount Lath and Plaster Co.'s Agent of Process on April 13, 2018.
Default as to defendant Kehoe Plastering Company, Inc. was entered on July 18, 2018. Statement of Damages filed July 18, 2018.
Default as to defendant Paramount Lath & Plastering Co. entered July 24, 2018. Statement of Damages filed July 24, 2018.
Plaintiff's Summons, Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages was personally served on defenant RDI Corporation's Agent of Process on October 16, 2018.
Default as to defendant RDI Corporation (f/k/a Reeder Development Corporation) was entered on January 14, 2019. Statement of Damages was filed on January 14, 2019.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on February 11, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Nathan Talley, and against the defaulted defendants Kehoe Plastering Company, Inc., Paramount Lath and Plaster Co. and RDI Corporation (f/k/a Reeder Development Corporation), as follows:

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Nevin, Esq., counsel for Plaintiff Thomas Sinclair; Andrew Sharp, Esq., ccounsel for remaining Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co.

Court and counsel review the facts to support consolidation of the three cases: Sinclair, Talley, and Draper. Mr. Nevin represents that none of the Plaintiffs are well enough to testify at trial and their testimony will be presented by video deposition and deposition transcript reading. Counsel advise the Court as to which experts will be testifying in each case, to what extent they overlap, and the general extent of their anticipated testimony. The Court examines Mr. Sharp as to what prejudice will occasion his client if the cases are consolidated, particularly with respect to the jury's ability to distinguish the injuries and causation as to the three plaintiffs.

After further discussion, the Court will tentatively consolidate the three cases pending the determination of the appropriate standard of causation [e.g. "but for" vs. "substantial factor"] to be determined before trial. Mr. Nevin is to prepare an Order provisionally consolidating 276622, 276623, and 276633. Final determination of consolidation will be determined at hearing on August 19, 2019.

Court and counsel discuss additional matters to be resolved pre-trial in motions in limine, including the scope of page/line designations. Court recesses for counsel to determine the format and timing of the pre-trial motions.

After recess, counsel advise the Court that they will address all pre-trial issues by motions in limine to be filed by August 7, 2019 with opposition due by August 12, 2019. No replies will be filed. Page/line deignations will be filed by August 5, 2019, opposition by August 12, 2019, and reply, if any, by August 14, 2019. Hearing on all motions and page/line designations will be August 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m.

The Court will allow exhibit lists and witness lists to be filed separately for each case. All pre-trial motions shall be filed in one case only but may address each case individually as necessary.

Counsel request the use of a jury questionnaire which the Court allows and which will be reviewed and finalized at hearing on August 19, 2019. The Court advises counsel that it will not accept any settlements in any of the three cases after August 19, 2019, 3:29 p.m. Jury selection will commence on August 20, 2019 and will proceed to verdict notwithstanding settlement.

Court adjourns.

Judge: Hon. Harold Kahn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D505)

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
